channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Pete Carroll introduced as Raiders head coach
item.description: Pete Carroll, the man chosen to lead the Las Vegas Raiders into the 2025-26 season after a 4-13 record in their previous campaign, is set to be welcomed at a news conference Monday.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, Fox 5

item.title: Trump to sign executive order banning transgender people from military service
item.description: A fact sheet for Monday’s executive order refers to transgender Americans as “trans-identifying,” a technical term used by some to delegitimize transgender identities.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Starbucks offering free refills to all customers, bringing back condiment bar
item.description: Starbucks is implementing new and old policies at its cafes in the United States and Canada to improve the customer experience. Beginning on Monday, customers who order a “for here” beverage can also get free refills of hot or iced coffee or hot or iced tea during their visit.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Person seen firing gun into air amid crowd of Philadelphia Eagles fans; police investigating
item.description: Video of the alleged incident, shared to X late on Sunday, appeared to show a large crowd celebrating the Eagles' win when a person raised the gun into the air.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KREX 5

item.title: What to know about the escalating conflict in eastern Congo as rebels close in on Goma
item.description: DAKAR, Senegal (AP) — Fighting has escalated sharply in recent weeks in eastern Congo, where rebels have seized key towns and are closing in on the city of Goma, the government’s last stronghold in the region bordering Rwanda.
item.dc:creator: MONIKA PRONCZUK and MARK BANCHEREAU, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Taylor Swift watches boyfriend Travis Kelce and the Chiefs beat the Bills and get back to Super Bowl
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Mo. (AP) — You could say that Taylor Swift's championship era has reached its second consecutive season.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Southern California rain helps firefighters but creates risk of flooding and toxic ash runoff
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Rain fell on parts of Southern California on Sunday and the scattered showers were expected to continue overnight, boosting the risk of toxic ash runoff in areas scorched by Los Angeles-area wildfires. Flood watches were in effect through 4 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Percival Everett's 'James' awarded Carnegie Medal for fiction
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — For author Percival Everett, libraries have long been a source of knowledge and discovery and pleasure, even of the forbidden kind.
item.dc:creator: HILLEL ITALIE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Top Trump administration officials in Chicago for start of immigration enforcement crackdown
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Top Trump administration officials, including “border czar” Tom Homan and the acting deputy attorney general, visited Chicago on Sunday to witness the start of ramped-up immigration enforcementin the nation’s third-largest city as federal agencies touted arrests around the country.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: ICE agents carry out operation in San Jose, mayor says
item.description: SAN JOSE, Calif. (KRON) -- U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) were in San Jose conducting an operation on Sunday, Mayor Matt Mahan's office confirmed in a statement sent to KRON4. The San Jose Police Department was notified that ICE agents were in the city.
item.dc:creator: Jack Molmud, NewsNation

item.title: Researchers studying whether medical cannabis could help late-stage dementia patients
item.description: As research continues, those involved say this would be a game changer, especially for patients with very limited options.
item.dc:creator: Josh Shortt, Fox 5

item.title: Bills cornerback Christian Benford ruled out of AFC title game vs. Chiefs with a concussion
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Mo. (AP) — Buffalo Bills cornerback Christian Benford was ruled out of the AFC title game against the Kansas City Chiefs on Sunday night with a concussion.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: A Sundance documentary called ‘The Stringer’ disputes who took AP’s 'napalm girl' photo in Vietnam
item.description: The authorship of The Associated Press’s renowned “napalm girl” photograph is being called into question in the new documentary “The Stringer,” which premiered at the Sundance Film Festival in Utah.
item.dc:creator: Lindsey Bahr, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Sen. Marshall 'absolutely' comfortable with Trump’s purge of inspectors general
item.description: Sen. Roger Marshall (R-Kan.) suggested that, like Reagan, Trump could potentially rehire some of the watchdog officials after cleaning house at the agencies.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Husband of nurse, mom of three found dead at bottom of staircase went for beer after finding body: police
item.description: Authorities are investigating the death of a Utah nurse, whose husband allegedly told police he went out to get beer after finding her body at the bottom of a stairwell.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Acting deputy AG head visits Chicago to 'observe' immigration enforcement
item.description: Chicago residents, especially in immigrant circles, have already been on edge for months in anticipation of large-scale immigration arrests promised by the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Michigan cadet disarms 5-year-old with gun while serving subpoena
item.description: The cadet asked the child to have an adult come to the door. When the child returned they were armed with a loaded handgun.
item.dc:creator: Corinne Moore, NewsNation

item.title: Freighter on the move after it was freed from ice on frozen Lake Erie
item.description: BUFFALO, N.Y. (AP) — A freighter was on its way to Canada on Sunday after it was freed from ice that trapped it in a frozen Lake Erie for days, the U.S. Coast Guard said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: As LA schools prepare to reopen, toxic ash raises concerns
item.description: As parents in Los Angeles County prepare to send their children back to the schools in wildfire impacted areas that are still standing, parents are wondering how safe they are.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Private investigator first to admit guilt in offering bribes to get intoxicated drivers off the hook
item.description: A private investigator embroiled in a corruption scandal that rocked New Mexico’s law enforcement community for over a decade was the first to plead guilty to a slew of federal charges that include racketeering and bribery, according to court records released Friday.
item.dc:creator: SEJAL GOVINDARAO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 4 tips for launching a side hustle at any age
item.description: Either start your own business or join an existing side-hustle platform that matches people with opportunities.
item.dc:creator: Kimberly Palmer, KTLA 5

item.title: The only person in the world with a functioning pig organ is thriving after a record 2 months
item.description: An Alabama woman passed a major milestone Saturday to become the longest living recipient of a pig organ transplant – healthy and full of energy with her new kidney for 61 days and counting.
item.dc:creator: LAURAN NEERGAARD, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Aurora borealis returns: When and where to watch the northern lights
item.description: Sky gazers in several U.S. states could get a colorful glimpse of the northern lights as we enter the weekend, thanks to a recent geomagnetic storm.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KRON4

item.title: Trump's attempt to end birthright citizenship would overturn more than a century of precedent
item.description: President Donald Trump has said since his first administration that he wants to end birthright citizenship, a constitutional right for everyone born in the United States. This week he issued an executive order that would eliminate it, upending more than a century of precedent.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM LEE BREWER and JANIE HAR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Las Vegas Raiders agree to hire 73-year-old Pete Carroll as their head coach, AP source says
item.description: Pete Carroll has a youthful energy that many people half his age don't possess, and the Raiders hope that enthusiasm — and, more importantly, his proven track record — translates to Las Vegas.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI and MARK ANDERSON, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Man says he was behind some of the viral googly eyes on public art in Oregon
item.description: PORTLAND, Ore. (AP) — A resident of the central Oregon city of Bend says he was the person behind some of the googly eyes that appeared on sculptures around the city in recent months and sparked a viral sensation widely covered by news outlets.
item.dc:creator: CLAIRE RUSH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Brawl over Pokémon cards breaks out at California Costco
item.description: The fight, captured on video by X user DisguisedToast, unfolded on Jan. 16 at the Costco in Atwater Village, the Los Angeles Times reported.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, Fox 5
